title: Login PRD
subtitle: v1 · B2C
context: Email-first signup, magic-link primary. Token TTL, session model, and SSO scope are undecided and need to converge before implementation.

decisions.question: Which auth methods for v1?
decisions.recommendation_reason: Spec twice mentions 'low friction' and a B2C audience; magic link hits both. Passwords drag in recovery flows, SSO drags in integration cost — neither worth it for v1.

options.label: Password + magic link
options.reason: Only worth it under a compliance mandate.

decisions.question: Password policy?
decisions.recommendation_reason: Only in scope once passwords exist; a moderate policy balances security and friction.

decisions.question: Check passwords against a breached-credential database?
decisions.recommendation_reason: Strict policy implies you care about credential stuffing; HIBP-style checks are cheap.

decisions.question: Session model?
decisions.recommendation_reason: Spec implies a single-page web client with no third-party API consumers yet, so a server session is simpler and revocable.

options.label: Server session + httpOnly cookie
options.reason: Revocable, simplest for a first-party web client.

options.label: Stateless JWT
options.reason: Only worth it with multiple resource servers.
